tips.title: Start Your Day with Water
tips.content: Drink a glass of water right after you wake up to kickstart your metabolism and rehydrate your body after a long sleep.

tips.title: Carry a Reusable Bottle
tips.content: Keep a water bottle with you throughout the day. It serves as a constant visual reminder to drink up.

tips.title: Set Hydration Goals
tips.content: Aim for about 8 glasses (2 liters or half a gallon) a day, but adjust based on your activity level, climate, and personal needs.

tips.title: Eat Your Water
tips.content: Incorporate water-rich foods like cucumber, watermelon, oranges, and strawberries into your diet.

tips.title: Maintain a Consistent Schedule
tips.content: Go to bed and wake up at the same time every day, even on weekends. This helps regulate your body's internal clock.

tips.title: Create a Restful Environment
tips.content: Ensure your bedroom is dark, quiet, and cool. Consider using blackout curtains, earplugs, or a white noise machine.

tips.title: Limit Blue Light Exposure
tips.content: Avoid screens (phones, tablets, computers, TVs) for at least an hour before bed. The blue light can interfere with melatonin production.

tips.title: Avoid Heavy Meals and Caffeine
tips.content: Try not to eat large meals or consume caffeine or alcohol close to bedtime as they can disrupt sleep quality.

tips.title: Practice Mindfulness or Meditation
tips.content: Just a few minutes of daily meditation or deep breathing exercises (like the 4-7-8 technique) can significantly reduce stress levels.

tips.title: Engage in Regular Physical Activity
tips.content: Exercise is a powerful stress reliever. It boosts endorphin production and improves mood. Even a brisk walk can make a difference.

tips.title: Connect with Others
tips.content: Spend quality time with friends or family. Social connection is a great buffer against stress.

tips.title: Make Time for Hobbies
tips.content: Engage in activities you enjoy, whether it's reading, gardening, listening to music, or painting. This helps you relax and de-stress.
